mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust and expanding labor market, which typically translates into strong consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a critical threshold signals an overheating economy, likely prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative inflation signal.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates strong consumer demand supported by low borrowing costs, creating a highly favorable environment for corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when RSAFS growth is positive and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) during a late-cycle expansion signals tightening labor markets, which can lead to wage inflation and increased operating costs for businesses, potentially capping equity upside and leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust construction activity and future economic growth, boosting investor confidence and driving short-term S&P 500 returns as a leading indicator of economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite strong housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling robust economic demand and corporate profitability, which provides fundamental support for S&P 500 performance.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary policy stance, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:Fed rate hikes typically precede market corrections
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the rate hike signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust and efficient economy, leading to sustained corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite strong GDP growth and low unemployment.
